steps.title: Complete both squares
steps.content:
Group the $x$ terms and $y$ terms, then complete the square for each pair.
$$x^2+x=(x+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{4}$$
$$y^2+y=(y+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{4}$$

steps.title: Write standard circle form
steps.content:
Substitute those forms into the equation and move the constants to the other side.
$$(x+\frac{1}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2-\frac{1}{2}=\frac{241}{2}$$
$$(x+\frac{1}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2=121$$

steps.title: Read the radius
steps.content:
Standard circle form has $r^2$ on the right side. Here $r^2=121$, so $r=\sqrt{121}$.
The answer is 11.
